ideal_answer_summary: Software engineering is the systematic process of designing developing testing and maintaining software systems.
A programming language is used to write instructions that computers can execute.
Object oriented programming organizes code using objects and core principles.
A variable stores data values in memory.
A function is a reusable block of code that performs a task.
Debugging is identifying and fixing errors in code.
Version control tracks changes in source code over time.
Git is a distributed version control system.
An algorithm is a step by step procedure to solve a problem.
A data structure organizes and stores data efficiently.
A loop repeats a block of code until condition is met.
Conditional statements execute code based on conditions.
API enables communication between software systems.
A database stores and manages data.
SQL is used to interact with relational databases.
HTTP is protocol for communication between client and server.
Frontend development focuses on user interface.
Backend handles server side logic and database interactions.
Compilation converts source code into machine code.
Interpreter executes code line by line.
Testing ensures software works as expected.
Unit test checks individual components.
A framework provides predefined structure and tools.
A library is collection of reusable code.
Cloud computing provides computing services over internet.
Deployment is releasing application to production.
Code review improves quality through peer feedback.
Refactoring improves code without changing behavior.
Exception handling manages runtime errors gracefully.
Open source software allows public access to source code.
OOP principles include encapsulation inheritance polymorphism and abstraction.
Time complexity measures algorithm performance using Big O notation.
Space complexity measures memory usage of algorithm.
Recursion is function calling itself with base condition.
Multithreading allows concurrent execution of tasks.
REST is stateless architecture using HTTP methods.
Indexing improves database query speed.
Deadlock occurs when processes wait indefinitely for resources.
Design patterns provide reusable solutions to common problems.
SOLID principles improve maintainability of OOP systems.
Microservices divide application into independent services.
CI CD automates testing and deployment pipelines.
Caching stores frequently used data to improve speed.
Load balancing distributes traffic across servers.
Containerization packages applications with dependencies.
ORM maps database tables to objects.
Event driven architecture reacts to events asynchronously.
Rate limiting restricts excessive API requests.
Memory management allocates and frees memory efficiently.
Scalability is ability to handle increased load.
TCP is reliable connection oriented while UDP is faster connectionless.
Authentication verifies identity authorization grants access.
Message queues enable asynchronous communication.
Graph database stores data as nodes and relationships.
MVC separates logic presentation and data.
Dependency injection improves modularity.
Garbage collection frees unused memory automatically.
Encryption secures data using algorithms.
Distributed systems consist of multiple connected systems.
WebSocket enables real time communication.
Design includes scalable infrastructure caching and optimized databases.
CAP theorem states distributed systems can guarantee only two properties.
